concept: Amused / Delighted by Something Small / Entertained Without Being Told To Be
description: Finding something quietly funny or entertaining: an old mistake that looks lighter in hindsight, a private joke, a thought that catches its own absurdity. Related to siora (joy) but narrower: siora is felt happiness in general, monelu is specifically the lighter, laughing-adjacent pleasure of being entertained, whether or not it reaches hola (laugh) itself.
sound_symbolism: 'Mo' opens warm and internal, a nasal held rather than released. 'Ne' continues the same internal warmth. 'Lu' opens it outward, the held amusement finally letting go.
grammatical_notes: Functions as an adjective: predication 'mia monelu nai' (I am amused). The quality noun, amusement, comes by rule: 'mia monelu phaelo' (I feel amusement). Related: siora (joy, the broader feeling), hola (laugh, the act it can lead to).

pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: A light, undemanding pleasure that needs nothing from the world to justify it is close to non-attachment: amusement asks to be noticed, not kept.
pillars.art-nouveau-aesthetics: Delight taken in something small and unforced is the same unhurried pleasure the movement's own ornament rewards a slow look with.

semantic_domains.emotion: The lighter feeling laughter sometimes follows
